title: Assembly Election LIVE: TVK chief Vijay files nomination for Tiruchi East; holds a roadshow

title: Mysuru railway division registers record passenger revenue, strong commercial growth

title: PM Modi visit on April 3: Red zones declared in parts of Chennai; drone ban enforced
content: Officials said that the restrictions will be in force for two days, April 3 and 4, during which flying of drone cameras and other unmanned aerial objects will be strictly prohibited in the designated areas

content: The AAP currently has 10 members in the Rajya Sabha, including seven from Punjab and three from Delhi

title: Kerala polls: Congress-led UDF manifesto promises ‘Indira guarantees’, five ‘dream’ projects and boost for health, higher education sectors
content: Five guarantees include free bus travel for all women in KSRTC buses, ₹1,000 monthly financial aid for college-going girl students, increase in welfare pension to ₹3,000 per month, ₹25-lakh health insurance cover for every household and interest-free loans up to ₹5 lakh for the youth to start own business

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Prakash Karat says ‘nothing wrong’ in SDPI backing LDF as tactical move
content: Senior CPI(M) leader says CPI(M)’s ‘policy is clear in not having any understanding with organisations that are communal or extremist in character’ and that there is ‘no personality cult’ in the party

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: What is the share of seats for parties in AIADMK-led National Democratic Alliance?
content: Of the 234 Assembly constituencies in the State, the AIADMK will contest in 167 constituencies, while the remaining seats have been allotted to its alliance partners

title: ‘Patriot’ postponed: Mammootty-Mohanlal movie gets new release date
content: The highly anticipated Malayalam film ‘Patriot’, featuring the legendary duo Mohanlal and Mammootty, has pushed its release date thanks to some “unforeseen circumstances,”

title: FIR against Rajasthan MLA Ravindra Singh Bhati for threatening folk singer Rawna
content: Folk singer Chotu Singh Rawna lodged the FIR against the Sheo MLA and five others after a controversy erupted over a comment on social media

content: He said that during the DMK rule, in 86% of cases involving crimes against women and children, chargesheets were filed within 60 days

content: Ruling front’s manifesto outlines a 60-point programme containing 950 proposals to create a ‘Nava Kerala’ (New Kerala), while seeking a mandate to continue ongoing development works

title: Secretive jungle cats need habitats outside protected areas: study
content: A new study’s finding that jungle cats use agricultural landscapes aligns with previous knowledge of the species. In and around farms, these cats keep rodent populations in check, thus ‘protecting’ crops. However, these landscapes lie outside protected areas and harbour several threats, including fragmented habitats and speeding vehicles

title: West Bengal's existence at stake due to ‘infiltration’, says Amit Shah
content: A storm is raging everywhere in Bengal; a storm for Poriborton (change) and say goodbye to Mamata Banerjee, says Union Home Minister Amit Shah

title: Traffic diverted from old bridge to new bridge across Kabini river on Mysuru-Nanjangud stretch of NH 766
content: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) sought the diversion of all vehicular traffic from the old bridge to the newly constructed bridge citing the need for repair and reconstruction of the old bridge, which is in a deteriorated condition

title: Suvendu Adhikari files nomination from Nandigram, says BJP will register decisive victory in Bengal
content: The BJP has fielded him from the two seats in a high-profile electoral battle against the ruling Trinamool Congress

title: Uttar Pradesh top performer under PM’s solar power scheme: UPNEDA
content: As per the national portal, Uttar Pradesh has achieved 4,48,233 cumulative rooftop solar installations, placing it third in India

title: Belagavi entrepreneur helps commercial units overcome LPG shortage using alternative energy sources
content: Belagavi-based innovator and entrepreneur turns disseminator of technology developed by premier institutions

title: Buyers should be cautious while buying gold: Legal Metrology Department official
content: Legal Metrology Department’s Deputy Commissioner said the buyers should insist for separate weighing for precious stones before their insertion to gold ornaments

title: Only Hindus, Sikhs, and Buddhists must be allowed to contest in SC-reserved constituencies: plea in Madras HC
content: Arjun Sampath of Indu Makkal Katchi also claims to have reliably learnt that 90% of nominations filed so far in the 44 reserved constituencies in Tamil Nadu are by Christians

title: Bombay High Court dismisses Anita Advani’s plea seeking recognition of ‘marriage’ with Rajesh Khanna
content: The Bombay High Court rejected a plea by actor Anita Advani that sought legal recognition of her relationship with the late actor Rajesh Khanna as a ‘marriage’

title: Samajwadi Party raises Mahabodhi temple management issue in Rajya Sabha, demands Buddhist control
content: Expressing concern over financial management, Ramji Lal Suman said the temple generates significant revenue but raised questions about the administration of its funds.

title: Government exempts Customs Duty for 40 petrochemical products amid West Asia crisis
content: “This measure has been taken as a temporary and targeted relief in order to ensure continued availability of critical petrochemical inputs for domestic industry, reduce cost pressures on downstream sectors, and safeguard supply stability in the country,” said an official press statement.

title: Game plan of the BJP is to impose President’s rule in Bengal says Mamata on Malda incident
content: Seven judicial officers were gheraoed by locals at a BDO office at Mothabari in the State’s Malda District on April 1; the officials were rescued later by a huge contingent of police and security forces.

title: Why is India pushing piped gas now? | Explained
content: Can PNG replace LPG for cooking? Why has LPG been preferred so far? How is LNG transported and used? What is holding back PNG expansion? Can domestic production meet demand? Will PNG reduce LPG import dependence?

title: Amendment to FCRA a direct attack on minority institutions: T.N. CM Stalin
content: “Despite stepping back, for now, due to Opposition protests and the upcoming elections in Kerala, where Christians live in large numbers, there are clear plans to push the FCRA [amendment] through a special session of Parliament,” Mr. Stalin said

title: GE Aerospace delivers sixth F404-IN20 engine for Tejas LCA-Mk1A
content: There has been a delay in the delivery schedule of engines for the LCA Mk-1A variant, which was criticised by Chief of Air Staff Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh during the last edition of the Aero India in Bengaluru

title: Lok Sabha passes Bill recognising Amaravati as sole capital of Andhra Pradesh
content: The bill, which will scuttle any future bid to alter the decision to make Amaravati the sole and permanent capital, was passed in the Lok Sabha by a voice vote

title: Doctors advise caution on vision risks linked to diabetes, weight-loss drugs
content: Ophthalmologists point to growing evidence that the GLP-1 class of drugs may be linked to an initial worsening of diabetic retinopathy and, in rare cases, non-arteritic ischemic optic neuropathy

title: Modified UDAN scheme will make air travel more affordable, says Vijayawada Airport Director
content: The scheme is not intended to support larger airports, and therefore, Vijayawada is not expected to receive much benefits from it

title: Punjab BJP working president visits blast site outside party office in Chandigarh
content: Police cordoned off the area and deployed personnel in the area, while a team of the National Investigation Agency (NIA) inspected the site to investigate the explosion

title: Need to continuously update public services to achieve goal of 'Viksit Bharat': PM Modi
content: In a video message at the launch of 'Sadhana Saptah', Prime Minister Modi said that the country's governance should improve the ease of living and quality of life for citizens daily.

title: Not mandatory to install pre-paid power meters: Centre in Lok Sabha
content: Responding to supplementaries during Question Hour, Power Minister Manohar Lal Khattar rejected suggestions that the government was compelling consumers to adopt pre-paid meters in a manner similar to private companies.

title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill and the question of identity: Who gets to define a trans life?
content: As India tightens its transgender law, love, desire, and dating apps reveal how trans people are still asked for proof before acceptance

title: India's defence exports hit record ₹38,424 crore in FY 25-26
content: The "big jump" in the defence exports reflected the growing "global trust" in India's indigenous defence capabilities, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said in a social media post, adding the country is "scripting an impressive defence exports success story."

title: Argentina football team ‘deceived’ Kerala by not coming to play: Sports Minister Abdurahiman
content: ‘After receiving the money, Argentine football team cheated us. We did not expect such a betrayal from them,’ says Minister

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Can Ganesh Kumar’s personal bastion of Pathanapuram be breached this time?
content: Transport Minister K.B. Ganesh Kumar is seeking his 6th term from the Pathanapuram Assembly constituency. The United Democratic Front has once again fielded Kerala Pradesh Congress Committee general secretary Jyothikumar Chamakkala, who managed to reduce Ganesh Kumar’s previously massive majority to 14,336 votes in the last election

title: Probe ordered into death of tigress in U.P.’s Dudhwa buffer zone
content: Daya Shankar, a member of the postmortem panel, told PTI that severe head injuries and internal bleeding led to the death of the tigress

title: Overcrowding Odisha jails worsens health and hygiene for prisoners, says CAG report
content: In all the 87 jails, as against the requirement of 2,203 bathing places, only 916 places were available for bathing

title: One killed, 20 injured as trolley carrying devotees overturns in U.P.’s Kushinagar
content: The incident occurred near the Siswa regulator under the Khadda police station limits at around 8 p.m. on April 1, when the trolley, which was transporting nearly 25 devotees, plunged into the canal

title: Church’s opposition to FCRA Bill triggers political storm in Kerala
content: With Assembly election round the corner, BJP goes into firefighting mode; the party’s Kerala president says Centre will factor in the Church’s reservations before tabling the proposed law in Parliament

title: Maharashtra Government rolls out NCMC card scheme providing travel concessions to senior citizens
content: Under this initiative, Aadhaar-linked NCMC smart cards will be distributed to students, senior citizens, persons with disabilities, women, and other concession beneficiaries

content: In the first phase, comprehensive information on the condition of housing, household amenities and assets possessed will be collected, the Home Ministry said

content: A team from a Mission Rabies centre traced the infection to a domesticated dog buried by its owner in Lanka, about 190 km from Guwahati

content: A collection of interviews by ‘The Hindu’ with key candidates and political leaders as part of Kerala Assembly elections 2026 coverage

title: Medical services under Dr. NTR Vaidya Seva to remain suspended until dues are cleared
content: Over 600 private and 200 government network hospitals in the State provide cashless treatments to people under the scheme, which was earlier known as Aarogyasri; the treatment cost is reimbursed by the government

content: A collection of stories on some of the key constituencies in Kerala that are set to elect their representatives to Kerala Legislative Assembly in 2026

content: The police arrested an extortionist belonging to the PREPAK from Thongkhong Laxmi Bazaar in Imphal West district

content: Petition filed by a Thrissur native alleges Suresh Gopi and his election agent indulged in ‘corrupt’ practices, as mentioned in Representation of People’s Act, to solicit votes

title: Wemby stays hot, doubles-down on MVP chase
content: After putting up 41 points and 18 rebounds in a runaway win, Spurs center Victor Wembanyama reiterated that the MVP award is squarely in his sights.

title: Rams receiver Nacua is in rehab, attorney says
content: Rams wide receiver Puka Nacua has checked in to rehab, his attorney told The California Post. Last month, a woman in Los Angeles filed a lawsuit against Nacua alleging he made an "unprovoked antisemitic statement" and later bit her on the shoulder.

title: Woods can leave U.S. for treatment, judge rules
content: A Florida judge granted Tiger Woods' request on Wednesday to travel outside the U.S. and enter a comprehensive inpatient treatment facility.

title: Kansas coach Self to return for 2026-27 season
content: Kansas men's basketball coach Bill Self is set to return for the 2026-27 season, saying in a statement Wednesday that he remains "focused and committed to Kansas" and "competing for a National Championship."

title: QB Mendoza puts on show at Indiana pro day
content: Expected No. 1 pick Fernando Mendoza threw roughly 56 passes in front of scouts from all 32 NFL teams and more than 100 members of the media Wednesday.

title: NCAA proposes ban on players who stay in draft
content: The NCAA Division I Cabinet will consider proposed changes to eligibility rules, including one that would bar athletes who have entered and remained in a professional sports draft from competing in college.

title: Sources: Rapper J. Cole to play pro hoops in China
content: Rap megastar J. Cole has signed a contract to play in the Chinese Basketball Association with the Nanjing Monkey Kings, sources told ESPN on Wednesday.

content: Lakers? Cavs? Warriors? A wild card? Before LeBron makes his decision this summer, sources around the league weigh in.

content: Roberto Martinez might think it's "worthless" to read into any results ahead of the World Cup, but alarm bells must surely be ringing in the USMNT camp over Christian Pulisic's 14-match goal drought.

content: Sjoeke Nusken's consolation goal for Chelsea proved to be too little, too late in loss to Arsenal in the UWCL.

content: Who are the favorites to win seasonlong honors? Here are the odds for MVP, Cy Young and Rookie of the Year in the AL and NL.

content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the fantasy baseball season.

title: Boxing divisional rankings: Moses Itauma crashes into heavyweight top 5
content: Moses Itauma disposed of Jermaine Franklin Jr. to move up at heavyweight. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal, Madrid eye young Leverkusen striker
content: Arsenal and Real Madrid are keen on Bayer Leverkusen striker Christian Kofane. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Why new rules have Max Verstappen on the brink of walking away from F1
content: Max Verstappen predicted F1's new rule set would underwhelm, and 2026 is proving him correct, so much so that he might just quit the sport entirely.

title: AAP removes Raghav Chadha as party's deputy leader in Rajya Sabha
content: The Aam Aadmi Party has requested the Rajya Sabha Secretariat to remove Raghav Chadha as Deputy Leader of the party in the Upper House.  The party also wants him excluded from speaking time allocated to AAP, proposing Ashok Mittal for the Deputy Leader role. This move indicates a significant internal leadership adjustment in Parliament, though official reasons remain undisclosed.

title: Judicial officers engaged in SIR gheraoed for hours: How Malda 'hostage' drama unfolded
content: The Supreme Court has taken serious note of the Malda hostage incident, issuing show-cause notices to West Bengal's chief secretary and DGP. Protesters, angered by voter list removals, gheraoed judicial officers for hours. The court termed the event a "deplorable" and "calculated" attempt to disrupt the electoral process, demanding central forces for officer safety.

title: IPL: After snub, RCB star heads to court to secure playing rights
content: Sri Lankan fast bowler Nuwan Thushara has taken Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) to court, seeking a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for the 2026 Indian Premier League. Thushara argues that SLC's refusal, citing fitness standards, is unreasonable as his contract ended and he informed them of his departure from international cricket.

title: Oracle investors remain uneasy with rising capex as company announces job cuts
content: Oracle is implementing significant layoffs across its global workforce to fund its ambitious AI data center expansion. This move aligns with its FY26 Restructuring Plan, aiming to improve profitability and productivity. The company is offering severance packages to affected employees, mirroring industry-wide investments in AI infrastructure.

title: Gold futures sink nearly 4% after Trump remarks on Iran; check rates in your city
content: Gold prices plummeted by Rs 6,004 to Rs 1,47,704 per 10 grams on Thursday, reversing a four-day rally. Intensifying West Asian geopolitical tensions and a strengthened US dollar, following President Trump's warnings of further action against Iran, dampened investor sentiment. This shift also saw traders fully price out US Federal Reserve interest rate cuts for 2026.

title: India scraps import tax on petrochemicals: Govt may lose over $190 mn in revenue
content: India has waived customs duties on 40 petrochemical products until June 2026, costing an estimated 18 billion rupees. This emergency measure aims to counter supply disruptions from the Middle East conflict, stabilize prices, and support key industries like plastics, pharmaceuticals, and textiles by ensuring raw material availability and reducing production costs.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company sets this severance condition for laid-off employees
content: Oracle has started its largest-ever layoffs, impacting thousands globally. Employees must sign termination documents via DocuSign to receive severance, which appears less generous than competitors.  These cuts, potentially reaching 30,000, aim to fund a massive AI data center expansion, a move that has placed the company under significant financial strain.

title: ‘Gym se body ban jayegi, bowling nahi’: Shami’s one line that sparked Mohsin Khan’s comeback
content: Mohsin Khan, 27, whose career has been marred by injuries, made his return to competitive cricket after a gap of 455 days. Since bursting onto the scene in IPL 2022, Mohsin has survived a career-threatening shoulder injury, a groin injury, and then a torn anterior cruciate ligament (ACL). On Wednesday, he showed a glimpse of his best in his opening spell against Delhi Capitals, which read 3-1-6-1.

title: KKR vs SRH, IPL 2026: 5 players to watch in today’s match
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face Sunrisers Hyderabad with their spinners needing to find form at Eden Gardens.  KKR's bowling depth is tested by injuries, while SRH's bowling faces scrutiny.  Key players like Abhishek Sharma and Varun Chakravarthy will be crucial in this high-stakes encounter.

title: Amazon cloud operations in Bahrain reportedly damaged in Iran strike
content: Amazon's cloud operations in Bahrain were reportedly damaged following an Iranian strike, with civil defense forces extinguishing a fire at a company facility. This incident follows threats from Iran's Revolutionary Guards against US companies in the Middle East. AWS facilities in the region have faced disruptions amid the ongoing conflict.

title: Who is Ashok Mittal? MP who replaces Raghav Chadha as AAP's deputy leader
content: The Aam Aadmi Party has requested the Rajya Sabha secretariat to remove Raghav Chadha as the party's deputy leader in the House and cease allocating him speaking time from AAP's quota. Sources indicate that Rajya Sabha MP Ashok Kumar Mittal, founder of Lovely Professional University, is being considered as his replacement.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 15: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 950 crore India net
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has made box office history, officially crossing the massive Rs. 900 crore India net milestone in just 14 days. The Aditya Dhar directorial, starring Ranveer Singh as the lethal spy Hamza, has collected a staggering Rs 922.26 crore domestic net so far. Despite the ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film continues to draw huge crowds, even outperforming the lifetime collections of the first 'Dhurandhar' in just 11 days. With a global haul of Rs' 1,466 crore, this action epic featuring Sanjay Dutt and Arjun Rampal is now rapidly chasing the elite Rs' 1,000 crore India club.

title: 'Minutes away from striking Pak from sea': Navy chief reveals key Op Sindoor moment
content: Indian Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi revealed the Navy was moments from striking Pakistan by sea during Operation Sindoor. Pakistan requested a halt to military actions. The operation, launched after the Pahalgam terror attack, targeted terrorist bases. The Navy's swift deployment and aggressive posture demonstrated its capabilities. Nine major terrorist camps were identified and struck with precision.

title: Dallas Cowboys and Kansas City Chiefs linked in bold first-round draft shake-up
content: The Dallas Cowboys are considering a proposed draft trade with the Kansas City Chiefs that would move them from pick No. 20 to No. 29 while gaining extra mid-round selections. The idea boosts depth, especially on defense, but risks missing elite talent. Dallas must decide between immediate impact players or building roster depth.

title: US brings EA-37B 'Compass Call' to Iran war: The jet that can jam communications, blind radar
content: The US Air Force has deployed its new EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft to the UK, bolstering operations against Iran. Spotted at RAF Mildenhall, these advanced planes are designed to disrupt enemy communications and radar systems, weakening their coordination.  With a crew of nine and cutting-edge technology, the EA-37B is a significant addition to ongoing military campaigns.

title: Celtics vs Heat injury update: Who's playing, injured, team stats, and more
content: The Boston Celtics, leading their division, play the Miami Heat at Kaseya Center. Miami needs this win to secure a play-in spot. Both teams have injury concerns. Boston has dominated the season series. The Celtics are favored to win, but Miami's home advantage and determination could make it a close contest.

title: 'Complete breakdown of law & order in Bengal': SC after judicial officers held hostage over SIR
content: The Supreme Court has strongly condemned the hostage situation involving judicial officers in Malda, issuing showcause notices to top West Bengal officials. The court cited a "complete breakdown of law and order" and an "abdication of duty," ordering central forces deployment and allowing probes into the incident. Leaders are urged to avoid politicizing the matter.

title: 'No identity within the team’: Ex-India batter blasts LSG, questions franchise direction
content: Abhinav Mukund has questioned Lucknow Super Giants' team structure and direction following their IPL 2026 defeat to Delhi Capitals. He highlighted a perceived lack of team identity compared to franchises like Gujarat Titans, suggesting LSG relies too heavily on individual brilliance rather than a defined playing style. Mukund also raised concerns about team balance and selection decisions.

title: To chase or to defend? What IPL 2026 early trends suggest after 1st round
content: IPL 2026 is witnessing a dominant trend of chasing teams emerging victorious in all five matches played so far. Despite varying total scores, from high-scoring contests to modest ones, the team batting second has consistently clinched the win. This pattern, particularly under lights, raises questions about the toss's growing importance and the potential challenges for bowlers as the tournament progresses.

title: Bengaluru techie dies by suicide after baby drowns in bucket
content: An eleven-month-old baby boy drowned in a bucket of water after his mother briefly left him unattended. Overwhelmed by guilt and unable to cope with the tragedy, the 29-year-old software professional died by suicide, leaving behind a note blaming herself for her son's death.

title: March PMI snapshot: India’s manufacturing growth slows to 53.9
content: India's manufacturing sector experienced a slowdown in March, with growth moderating due to rising costs, intense competition, and global uncertainties like the Middle East conflict. While new orders and output saw slower expansion, employment surged, and export demand remained robust, indicating a mixed but resilient industrial landscape.

title: Mohammed Shami scripts IPL history, sets big record despite LSG defeat
content: Mohammed Shami etched his name in IPL history by taking his fifth first-ball wicket, becoming the first bowler to achieve this feat. Despite his impressive performance, Lucknow Super Giants suffered a six-wicket defeat against Delhi Capitals. Shami, out of the national setup for over a year, reiterated his commitment to playing as long as he enjoys the game.

title: ‘Cheated us’: Messi, Argentina team face serious allegations from Kerala minister
content: Kerala Sports Minister V Abdurahiman has accused the Argentina national football team of betrayal, alleging they took a substantial payment of Rs 250 crore but failed to honor a commitment to visit the state. The minister expressed deep disappointment, stating that extensive planning and sponsorship efforts were made for the potential visit, which was expected to include Lionel Messi.

title: 'Ramayana' teaser: Ranbir Kapoor debuts as Lord Rama in epic first glimpse
content: The highly anticipated trailer for 'Rama,' a cinematic retelling of the 'Ramayana,' has officially debuted online, showcasing Ranbir Kapoor as Lord Rama. This visual spectacle, unveiled on Hanuman Jayanti, offers glimpses of Rama's life, from his exile to his reign as a crowned king.  The film, a two-part epic, also features Sai Pallavi as Sita and Yash as Ravana.

title: ‘Rotten tomatoes, obscene photos’: Dutch national's 'bizarre' parcel to B'luru airport
content: A 65-year-old Dutch national of Indian origin was arrested in Bengaluru for sending a parcel with unusual items, including rotten food and obscene photos, to the Kempegowda International Airport. The man claimed it was a ritual to prevent untoward incidents, stating he had sent similar parcels elsewhere. He has been remanded to judicial custody pending a medical examination.

title: Sanjiv Goenka–Rishabh Pant exchange goes viral after LSG loss, sparks fresh IPL storm
content: Lucknow Super Giants began IPL 2026 with a six-wicket loss to Delhi Capitals, exposing batting frailties as they were bowled out for 141. Owner Sanjiv Goenka's intense on-field discussion with captain Rishabh Pant after the match drew comparisons to a similar incident last season. Pant's decision to open the batting also came under scrutiny following his dismissal for 7.

title: Trump, Hegseth’s 'back to the Stone Age' threat to Iran draws swift consulate response
content: President Trump warned Iran would be hit "extremely hard" in the coming weeks, aiming to return the nation to the "Stone Age." This aggressive rhetoric follows ongoing hostilities and a shift in US stated goals, drawing sharp criticism from Iran's consulate in Mumbai. The consulate highlighted Iran's ancient heritage, asserting its existing greatness.

title: Artemis II astronauts manually pilot Orion: Nasa video shows spacecraft heading towards Moon
content: Nasa's Orion spacecraft has successfully separated from its rocket during the Artemis II mission. Astronauts are now conducting proximity operations tests. This historic journey marks humanity's return to lunar space after over 50 years. The mission, lasting about 10 days, will see the crew fly around the Moon and capture images of its surface.

title: IPL 2026: LSG vs DC turns into WWE Raw as fan brawl erupts in stands - watch
content: A fan brawl disrupted an IPL 2026 match. Delhi secured a six-wicket victory. The incident in the stands gained widespread attention. Viral footage showed the fight and police intervention. This event became a significant talking point. The match will be remembered for both the game and the disturbance.

title: VinFast MPV 7 bookings open in India: Launch date, token amount & more
content: Vietnamese automaker VinFast has opened bookings for its upcoming seven-seater electric MPV, the MPV 7, in India for a Rs 21,000 token. The company also plans to launch a fleet version, an electric cab service, and electric scooters later this year. The MPV 7, expected to rival the Kia Carens Clavis EV, boasts a 201 hp motor and a 60.

title: Bihar man kills pregnant wife over LPG refill money dispute
content: A pregnant woman in Begusarai was allegedly bludgeoned to death with bricks by her husband after an argument over him spending money meant for refilling an LPG cylinder on gambling. The victim, Lakshmi Kumari, was expecting her second child. Her husband, Raj Choudhary, has been arrested.

title: Centre waives duty on key petrochemical goods amid Middle East crisis: Check list
content: Amidst ongoing Middle East energy disruption, the government has announced a complete customs duty exemption on critical petrochemical products until June 30. This temporary measure aims to alleviate supply chain challenges, reduce cost pressures on downstream industries, and ensure the continued availability of essential inputs for various manufacturing sectors.
